interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces funding to support domestic critical mineral and material supply chains
interventions.description:
On 19 May 2026, the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation announced USD 19.3 million in grants to USA Rare Earth as part of the Critical Material Innovation, Efficiency, and Alternatives funding program. The funding will support the construction and operation of a pilot-scale continuous ion exchange (CIX) rare earth element production plant in Stillwater, Oklahoma.

The funding forms part of a broader DOE announcement of USD 45.7 million for 19 projects. The stated purpose of the measure is to address domestic critical minerals and materials supply chain gaps and reduce reliance on foreign sources of rare earth elements. The announcement notes that more than 95% of the U.S. supply of rare earth elements comes from foreign sources and frames the investment as a means to strengthen national security and energy independence.

interventions.title: Canada: Canada Growth Fund provides CAD 145 million in convertible notes to Sayona Inc for lithium mine expansion
interventions.description:
On 12 May 2026, the Canada Growth Fund (CGF) announced a commitment to invest up to CAD 145 million (approximately USD 105.43 million) in Sayona Inc to support the expansion of the North American Lithium (NAL) mine in Québec. The funding is provided via convertible notes and forms part of a larger CAD 417 million capital raise intended to enhance cost competitiveness and increase production capacity at Canada's largest operating lithium mine.

The financing is expected to support 252 jobs in the Abitibi region. A portion of the committed capital is contingent on the company meeting specific milestones, including permitting progress and expansion de-risking.

In this context, Yannick Beaudoin, President and CEO of CGF Investment Management Inc, stated that the project "reinforces Canada’s role in the North American battery materials value chain and supports jobs and industrial competitiveness in Québec." Lucas Dow, Managing Director and CEO of Sayona's parent company Elevra Lithium Limited, noted that the capital "helps de-risk the staged expansion and gives us greater certainty to deliver growth at NAL."

interventions.title: Argentina: RIGI benefits approved for "Expansion Phase 1B" project from Minera del Altiplano SA Sucursal Dedicada
interventions.description:
On 6 April 2026, the Ministry of Economy of Argentina published Resolution No. 431/2026 approving the "Expansion Phase 1B" as a beneficiary of the Incentive Regime for Large Investments (RIGI in Spanish). The project will be managed by Minera del Altiplano SA Sucursal Dedicada, a dedicated mining branch focused on lithium carbonate production. Under RIGI, the project will obtain tax benefits for five years. The effective inclusion date is 25 March 2026, hence the tax benefits will apply retroactively.

The tax benefits include a 5-year cap on the Income Tax of 25% (the normal rate progressively increases until 35%), and tax credits applicable to the VAT. Additionally, Minera del Altiplano SA Dedicated Branch will be able to import certain goods exempted from import duties and internal taxes (see related interventions).

The project is located in the Salar del Hombre Muerto, Catamarca Province, and involves expanding the installed lithium carbonate production capacity from 28'500 tonnes per year to 38'000 tonnes per year. According to the Resolution, the project will "expand the installed capacity of lithium carbonate production by nine thousand five hundred (9'500) additional tons per year".

interventions.title: France: Orano Hydro Battery Recycling receives EUR 82 million grant from the EU's Innovation Fund
interventions.description:
On 18 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 82 million (USD 94 million) financial grant for Orano Hydro Battery Recycling. The grant was provided for the company’s involvement in the “REC2pCAM” project in France, selected as a beneficiary of the EU’s Innovation Fund. Orano Hydro Battery Recycling is a subsidiary of the Orano group dedicated to recovering and purifying valuable materials from end-of-life electric vehicle batteries.

According to the project description, the REC2pCAM project aims to build an industrial-scale facility in France dedicated to producing high-quality precursor cathode active material (PCAM) for nickel-cobalt-manganese (NCM) batteries using entirely European-sourced recycled battery materials. At full capacity, the plant is expected to produce around 20'000 tonnes of PCAM annually, strengthening the European battery supply chain, reducing reliance on imported critical raw materials, and supporting the deployment of low-emission electric vehicles.

The EU's Innovation Fund

Funded by revenues from the auction of emission allowances from the EU’s Emissions Trading System, the Innovation Fund provides financial grants for the demonstration of innovative low-carbon technologies. It is exempted from state aid rules.

interventions.title: Denmark: Topsoe Battery Materials AS gets EUR 46.3 million grant from the EU's Innovation Fund
interventions.description:
On 18 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 46.3 million (USD 53.1 million) financial grant for Topsoe Battery Materials AS. The grant was provided for the company’s involvement in the “NEXTGEN CAM” project in Denmark, selected as a beneficiary of the EU’s Innovation Fund. Topsoe Battery Materials AS is a Danish company that develops and produces cobalt-free LNMO cathode active materials for next-generation lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles and energy storage.

According to the project description, NEXTGEN CAM will build an industrial-scale LNMO cathode active material plant in Sines, Portugal, producing a cobalt-free, next-generation battery material that outperforms existing NMC and LFP chemistries using fully electrified processes. The project aims to avoid 1.88 million tonnes of CO2e over its first decade and ambitions to anchor advanced battery materials manufacturing in Europe, reducing strategic dependencies and supporting the EU's goals under the European Green Deal and the Net Zero Industry Act.

The EU's Innovation Fund

Funded by revenues from the auction of emission allowances from the EU’s Emissions Trading System, the Innovation Fund provides financial grants for the demonstration of innovative low-carbon technologies. It is exempted from state aid rules.

interventions.title: Canada: CIB provides CAD 459 million joint loan to Nouveau Monde Graphite
interventions.description:
On 17 March 2026, Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) provided, together with Export Development Canada (EDC), a CAD 459 million (approx. USD 334.56 million) loan to Nouveau Monde Graphite (NMG). Nouveau Monde Graphite is a Canadian company developing fully integrated graphite operations for the battery and electric vehicle markets.

The funding supports the development, construction, and commissioning of the Matawinie Mine in Quebec. The mine was among the projects referred to the federal Major Projects Office by Prime Minister Carney in autumn 2025.

In this context, Ehren Cory, Chief Executive Officer, Canada Infrastructure Bank, said: "The CIB’s commitment of a debt facility to Nouveau Monde Graphite will advance the next phase of the Matawinie Mine toward final investment decision and construction. The CIB works to bridge financing gaps and accelerate timelines in large-scale critical minerals projects which are essential to Canada’s economic competitiveness".

Canada Infrastructure Bank

Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) is a federal financial agency investing in revenue-generating infrastructure across Canada. The CIB partners with private, public, and institutional investors to support its projects. It employs multiple funding tools including equity, loans, and derivatives.

interventions.title: Poland: Elemental Battery Metals gets EUR 150.7 million grant from the EU's Innovation Fund
interventions.description:
On 17 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 150.7 million (USD 173.9 million) financial grant for Elemental Battery Metals. The grant was provided for the company’s involvement in the “Polvolt” project in Poland, selected as a beneficiary of the EU’s Innovation Fund. Elemental Battery Metals is a Polish special-purpose company being developed to refine critical metals from recycled battery black mass and electronic waste.

According to the project description, POLVOLT aims to establish a major European facility for recycling and producing critical raw materials from battery black mass, electronic waste, and copper-bearing scrap. The project will recover precious metals, base metals, and battery metals such as nickel, cobalt, manganese, and lithium using advanced pyrometallurgical and hydrometallurgical processes powered partly by on-site renewable energy. With a planned recycling capacity of around 80'000 tonnes, the facility is expected to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 86% compared to conventional production. The recovered materials will supply metal salts sufficient to produce batteries for about 450'000 electric vehicles annually. The project supports the EU Critical Raw Materials Act (see related state act) and Net Zero Industry Act by strengthening Europe’s battery materials supply chain and advancing the circular economy. It is also expected to create over 250 direct jobs in the Silesian region of Poland.

The EU's Innovation Fund

Funded by revenues from the auction of emission allowances from the EU’s Emissions Trading System, the Innovation Fund provides financial grants for the demonstration of innovative low-carbon technologies. It is exempted from state aid rules.

interventions.title: Finland: Fortum Battery Recycling Oy gets EUR 40 million grant from the EU's Innovation Fund
interventions.description:
On 15 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 40 million (USD 45.7 million) financial grant for Fortum Battery Recycling Oy. The grant was provided for the company’s involvement in the “NEXT HYDROMET” project in Finland, selected as a beneficiary of the EU’s Innovation Fund.

Fortum Battery Recycling Oy is a battery recycling company operating in Finland. The NEXT HYDROMET (Next-generational hydrometallurgical recycling of battery materials) project aims to demonstrate a breakthrough hydrometallurgical process for recycling lithium-ion batteries, integrating advanced hydrometallurgy with chromatographic separation and on-site electrochemical production of key process chemicals. The plant, located in Harjavalta, will process 3'000 tonnes of black mass per year, producing battery-grade nickel sulphate, cobalt sulphate, and lithium hydroxide, enough to supply materials for approximately 138'000 electric vehicles annually.

Operating at lower temperatures and powered by renewable electricity, the technology enables over 95% recovery of battery metals. Over its first ten years of operation, the project is expected to avoid approximately 103,253 tonnes of CO2-equivalent emissions.

The EU's Innovation Fund

Funded by revenues from the auction of emission allowances from the EU’s Emissions Trading System, the Innovation Fund provides financial grants for the demonstration of innovative low-carbon technologies. It is exempted from state aid rules.

interventions.title: Spain: EUR 200 million grant to expand EV battery, energy storage, and hydrogen manufacturing capacity
interventions.description:
On 5 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 200 million (USD 233 million) direct grant scheme from Spain. The measure will support investments in manufacturing capacity for electric-vehicle battery, energy storage, and hydrogen technologies. Aid under the scheme may be granted until 30 June 2026.

According to the press release, the scheme will also support the production of key components for these technologies, as well as the production or recovery of the critical raw materials required for them. The measure aims to support the transition to a net-zero economy.

The state aid is approved under the Clean Industrial Deal state aid framework (CISAF) of June 2025. The framework allows Member States to provide financial support for the development of clean energy and technology, as well as industrial decarbonisation (see related state act).

In this context, the Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition, Teresa Ribera, noted: "Spain’s new €200 million scheme will accelerate the production of batteries and energy storage for electric vehicles, as well as hydrogen technologies. Through direct grants, this measure strengthens Europe’s clean industrial transition while securing strategic supply chains. At a time of growing geopolitical uncertainty, Europe’s energy autonomy, reducing our dependence on imported fossil fuels, is more important than ever. Investing in batteries, storage and hydrogen is not only about competitiveness; it is about resilience and sovereignty. The funds will be deployed quickly so the battery industry can move ahead with this transition."

In its Decision, the Commission notes: "The measure is liable to distort competition, since it strengthens the competitive position of its beneficiaries. It also affects trade between Member States, since those beneficiaries are active in sectors in which intra-Union trade exists".

Regardless, it decided not to raise objections on the grounds that "it is compatible with the internal market pursuant to Article 107(3), point (c), of the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union."

The state aid is approved under the Clean Industrial Deal state aid framework (CISAF) of June 2025. The framework allows Member States to provide financial support for the development of clean energy and technology, as well as industrial decarbonisation (see related state act).

interventions.title: Canada: Government provides CAD 21.9 million to Mangrove Lithium for spodumene processing and lithium conversion project
interventions.description:
On 3 March 2026, the government of Canada announced it would provide CAD 21.9 (USD 16.06) million in funding to Mangrove Lithium for a project located in Delta, British Columbia. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take.

The investment supports the Spodumene Processing Pilot and Lithium Conversion Plant Engineering Study. According to the government description, the project aims to pilot the processing of spodumene concentrate alongside lithium refining to validate an end-to-end process for producing battery-grade lithium. Additionally, the funding will be used to complete early engineering work for a future lithium refinery of a larger scale. The approval of the funding is conditional upon the completion of final due diligence by the government.

In this context, Tim Hodgson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, said: “Canada is leveraging our critical mineral resources — including our lithium — to unlock supply chain security, job creation and clean energy innovation. Mangrove Lithium’s new headquarters will house North America’s first commercial electrochemical lithium refining facility — exactly the type of cutting-edge, sovereign Canadian project we need. By supporting projects like these, our new government is advancing Canada’s low-carbon potential, creating new careers, strengthening our security and creating reliable Canadian jobs in an uncertain time".

interventions.title: Brazil: FINEP announces BRL 200 million grant scheme for mineral transformation and urban mining
interventions.description:
On 6 February 2026, the Brazilian Company of Innovation and Research (FINEP) launched a BRL 200 million (USD 37.9 million) grant scheme. The scheme supports Brazilian companies' research and development projects in the mineral transformation sector. The scheme will be valid for one year.

According to Annexe 1 of the call, the following thematic lines will be supported under this call:

  • Line 1 – Critical and Strategic Minerals and Materials: Supports technological development of materials and products essential for energy transition and decarbonisation. Projects must go beyond basic mineral processing and involve transformation using at least one critical or strategic mineral.
  • Line 2 – Urban Mining: Supports recovery of critical and strategic materials from secondary sources such as electronic waste, batteries, and metallurgical residues. Focuses on high-value-added recovery processes.
  • Line 3 – Rare Earth Magnets: Supports the development and manufacturing of rare earth magnets.
  • Line 4 – Sustainable Mining Technologies: Supports environmentally sustainable mining technologies. Projects must address mine and tailings monitoring, decommissioning, or recovery of degraded areas.
  • Line 5 – Decarbonisation of Mineral Transformation: Supports low-carbon metallurgical processes and low-carbon cement solutions. Emphasises technologies such as electrolysis, low-carbon hydrogen, biomass, and CO₂ capture.

This call is part of a BRL 3.3 billion initiative from FINEP and the Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (MCTI), which includes other calls related to agro-industrial chains, health, infrastructure, digital transformation, energy transition, and national defence (see related state acts).

In this context, the president of Finep, Luiz Antônio Elias, said: “The purpose of this round of offering economic subsidies to companies is to contribute to fostering innovation, reducing regional asymmetries, promoting technology transfer, and strengthening national competitiveness, so that NIB's public policy achieves the expected results” (own translation).

interventions.title: United States of America: EXIM approves loan to establish a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve
interventions.description:
On 2 February 2026, the US government, through the Export-Import Bank of the United States, approved a Direct Loan of up to USD 10 billion to support Project Vault in the United States. The financing aims to establish a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve to secure access to essential raw materials for domestic manufacturers. The intervention targets US-based manufacturing supply chains and seeks to support production, processing, and storage of critical minerals across multiple states.

The approved loan will provide long-term financing to Project Vault, an independently governed public-private partnership involving original equipment manufacturers and private capital providers. According to the announcement, participating manufacturers reportedly include Clarios, GE Vernova, Western Digital, and Boeing, while suppliers include Hartree Partners, Mercuria Americas, and Traxys. The reserve is expected to store critical raw materials in facilities distributed across the United States to mitigate supply disruptions.

In this context, EXIM Chairman John Jovanovic said: “Project Vault is designed to support domestic manufacturers from supply shocks, support U.S. production and processing of critical raw materials, and strength America’s critical minerals sector. Thanks to President Trump’s leadership, the U.S. Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve will help manufacturers in the United States compete, grow, and lead globally while creating jobs domestically, strengthening our economy, and advancing the national interest.”

interventions.title: Canada: Canada Growth Fund provides USD 65 million in structured financing to Mangrove Lithium
interventions.description:
On 15 January 2026, the Canada Growth Fund (CGF) provided a USD 65 million structured financing package to Mangrove Lithium. The CGF did not specify the form of financing.

The state aid will support the commercialisation and deployment of an electrochemical lithium refining platform. Mangrove Lithium is a British Columbia-based cleantech company that has developed an electrochemical lithium refining technology to produce battery-grade lithium hydroxide and carbonate with lower carbon intensity than conventional methods, currently operating a 1'000-tonne facility and planning a 20'000-tonne Eastern Canada plant.

In this context, François-Philippe Champagne, Minister of Finance and National Revenue, said: "Investing in Canadian cleantech is an investment in our economic sovereignty, and is crucial to building a competitive, low-carbon economy. The funding announced today for Mangrove Lithium will help create here at home a future where innovation thrives, jobs are created, and global competitiveness is advanced".

Canada Growth Fund is a CAD 15 billion arm's-length investment vehicle that uses financing instruments to catalyse private capital investment in Canadian low-carbon projects, technologies, businesses, and supply chains.

interventions.title: Australia: Government increases Critical Minerals Facility budget to AUD 5.2 billion
interventions.description:
On 24 April 2025, the Australian Government, through the Department of Prime Minister and Cabinet, allocated an AUD 1.2 billion (USD 782 million) investment in the future Critical Minerals Strategic Reserve managed by the Critical Minerals Strategic Reserve Taskforce.

According to the release, the objective of the Critical Mineral Reserve is to maximise the strategic value of Australia's critical minerals. In this context, "Critical minerals and rare earths are essential inputs to the modern and future economy – underpinning the energy transition, high tech manufacturing, AI and the digital economy." The reserve is allocated from the 2025-26 Federal Budget.

On 12 January 2026, the Australian Government, through the Department of Industry, Science and Resources, announced an increase in the Critical Minerals Facility budget from AUD 4 billion to AUD 5.2 billion (USD 3.49 billion). The increase corresponds to the creation of the Critical Minerals Strategic Reserve with a budget of AUD 1.2 billion. According to the press release, the facility will provide loans for critical minerals projects.

In addition, the first minerals supported are antimony, gallium and rare earth elements. Equity support will also be provided (see related intervention).

The Facility is valid until 2031 or until funds run out.

interventions.title: Russia: Government exempts companies from the mineral extraction tax under certain conditions
interventions.description:
On 17 February 2023, the president of Russia signed federal law No.29 zeroing the mineral exploration tax (MET) for the companies engaged in MET of nickel, copper, and platinum-group metals in the Krasnoyarsk region. 

Specifically, the law makes the tax calculation coefficient zero from 1 January 2026 to 1 January 2038 for projects satisfying specific requirements stipulated by the law. The list includes the conclusion of an investment agreement with the Ministry of Finance and the Ministry of Natural Resources on the extraction of platinum group metals in the area. The local media reported that the firm eligible for the tax exemption is Russian Platinum, a Russian holding focused on producing platinum-group metals.

Previously in 2021, Russian Platinum's project in the region received government support in the form of the VEB.RF credit (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of War discloses funding to expand germanium and silicon optics production
interventions.description:
On 22 December 2025, the US Department of War announced providing USD 18.5 million under Defense Production Act Title III to Lattice Materials. The funding supports expanded production of germanium and silicon crystals at the company’s Bozeman, Montana facility. The announcement did not specify the type of funding.

According to the announcement, the funding targets the US defense industrial base and aims to strengthen the optics supply chain supporting multiple military platforms. The funding reportedly increases capacity for optical-grade germanium and silicon crystal growth and optics manufacturing. It also establishes domestic capability to produce germanium metal from recycled scrap. The funding seeks to reduce lead times for infrared lenses, windows, and mirrors used in surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting systems.

In this context, Assistant Secretary of War for Industrial Base Policy Mike Cadenazzi said: “Mitigating supply chain vulnerabilities and expanding domestic production for critical minerals is one of DOW's highest priorities. This investment will help ensure a secure supply of germanium and silicon-based optics for surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting capabilities that our warfighters need."

interventions.title: United States of America: U.S. Administration awards Crucible Metals LLC USD 210 million in funding for smelter and critical mineral processing facility in Tennessee
interventions.description:
On 15 December 2025, the U.S. Department of Commerce awarded Crucible Metals LLC USD 210 million in grants under the CHIPS Incentives Program (see related state act). The funding will support the construction of a smelter and critical minerals processing facility in Tennessee. The beneficiary operates in the mineral production.

According to the press release, the aid will support the production of the following critical and strategic minerals: gallium, bismuth, germanium, indium, antimony, tellurium, cadmium, palladium, copper, silver, gold, zinc, and lead, as well as sulfuric acid and semiconductor-grade sulfuric acid. Additionally, Korea Zinc has agreed to provide the United States Government and its customers priority access to its Korean production of 10 critical and strategic minerals starting in 2026.

In this context, Director of the CHIPS Program, Bill Frauenhofer, said: “These minerals and chemicals are essential components of the semiconductor supply chain, particularly for the manufacturing of silicon and compound semiconductor devices. This $6.6 billion domestic project, along with the United States’ expanded access to the Korea Zinc Onsan minerals production, will help alleviate potential chokepoints for U.S. companies".

interventions.title: Canada (Ontario): Government launches a CAD 500 million Critical Minerals Processing Fund
interventions.description:
On 12 December 2025, the government of Ontario officially launched the Critical Minerals Processing Fund (CMPF), a CAD 500 (USD 363.56) million financial support scheme aimed at expanding and modernising critical minerals processing and refining capacity in the province. The type of state aid that will be provided under the CMPF was not specified.

The fund was first announced in Ontario’s 2025 Budget and is administered by Invest Ontario, the province’s investment attraction agency, under the authority of the Ministry of Economic Development, Job Creation and Trade.

The CMPF will provide financial support to projects that enhance Ontario’s end-to-end critical minerals supply chain, with the stated objective of ensuring that minerals extracted in Ontario are also processed and refined domestically. The scheme is intended to support both the expansion of existing processing facilities and the construction of new mineral processing operations, particularly in Northern Ontario and the Ring of Fire region.

In this context, Vic Fedeli, Minister of Economic Development, Job Creation and Trade, said: “As global demand for critical minerals continues to rise, the Critical Minerals Processing Fund will help Ontario deliver as a trusted supplier. By building supply chains here at home, with Ontario workers, we are creating good jobs, strengthening communities, and reinforcing our province's reputation as a top destination for investment and growth".

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces funding to support rare earth element supply chains
interventions.description:
On 1 December 2025, the United States Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI) announced a funding opportunity of up to USD 134 million to strengthen domestic supply chains for rare earth elements (REEs). The initiative supports projects that can demonstrate the commercial viability of recovering and refining REEs from unconventional feedstocks, such as mine tailings, e-waste, and other waste materials. Funding will be provided through cooperative agreements and is open to, among others, for-profit entities.

According to the announcement, this funding opportunity aims to strengthen domestic supply chains by reducing U.S. reliance on foreign sources of rare earth elements. Its objectives are to:

• Support the design, construction, and operation of cost-effective demonstration processing lines at a new or existing facility capable of supplying 150–1000 metric tons per year of rare earth elements for domestic use;

• Require a single-site facility that can process, separate, and refine rare earth oxides into rare earth metals from feedstocks such as acid mine drainage, mine waste, deleterious materials, or e-waste, with priority given to heavy rare earth elements.

Funding will be provided through cooperative agreements and is open to, among others, for-profit entities. Individual awards are expected to range between USD 67 million and USD 134 million, with 1–2 awards anticipated. Applicants must provide a minimum 50% cost share of total project costs.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said: “For too long, the United States has relied on foreign nations for the minerals and materials that power our economy. We have these resources here at home, but years of complacency ceded America’s mining and industrial base to other nations. Thanks to President Trump’s leadership, we are reversing that trend, rebuilding America’s ability to mine, process, and manufacture the materials essential to our energy and economic security.”

A cooperative agreement is a federal funding mechanism similar to a grant but includes more active government participation, offering both financial support and technical guidance throughout the project period.

interventions.title: Russia: Government announces RUB 4 billion in grants to Amur Minerals LLC and Udokan Copper LLC
interventions.description:
On 24 November 2025, the government of Russia published Order No. 3351-р allocating up to RUB 2 billion (USD 24.7 million) in financial grant to Amur Minerals LLC, a Russian mining company developing the Malmyzhskoye copper-gold deposit and constructing an integrated mining.

According to the government order, the subsidy will finance the technological connection of the project to the regional power infrastructure operated by Rosseti. This support forms part of the federal project “New Opportunities for the Far East” under the state programme “Socioeconomic Development of the Far Eastern Federal District.” Funding is intended to reduce capital costs for critical infrastructure and help the company meet technical requirements necessary to bring the mining complex into full-scale operation.

In this context, Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin stated [translation]: “Such measures are necessary to encourage investors to increase capacity and launch new businesses in this strategically important macro-region for the entire country.”

interventions.title: Russia: Government announces RUB 4 billion in grants to Amur Minerals LLC and Udokan Copper LLC
interventions.description:
On 24 November 2025, the government of Russia published Order No. 3351-р allocating up to RUB 2 billion (USD 24.7 million) in financial grant to Udokan Copper LLC, a Russian copper mining company.

According to the official list attached to the order, the subsidy will support the construction of transport and storage facilities forming part of the operational infrastructure of the Udokan Mining and Metallurgical Plant. The support falls under the federal project “New Opportunities for the Far East” and aims to offset the high costs of building essential infrastructure in remote mineral-rich regions. The measure supports the expansion of ore processing capacity and improves logistical conditions for the movement of raw materials and finished products.

In this context, Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin stated [translation]: “Such measures are necessary to encourage investors to increase capacity and launch new businesses in this strategically important macro-region for the entire country.”

interventions.title: Canada: Government announced CAD 36.3 million in federal funding for Ucore's rare earth processing facility
interventions.description:
On 31 October 2025, Natural Resources Canada (NRCan), under its Global Partnerships Initiative (GPI), conditionally approved a non-repayable contribution of up to CAD 36.3 (USD 25.82) million to Ucore Rare Metals Inc for its “Pathway to Samarium and Gadolinium Security” project. The funding will support the demonstration and scale-up of a first-of-its-kind commercial processing facility in Kingston, Ontario, dedicated to the refining of the rare earth elements samarium and gadolinium using Ucore’s RapidSX™ separation technology.

According to the Canadian government, the project aims to establish North America’s first dedicated samarium and gadolinium oxide production facility, thereby strengthening Canada’s position in critical mineral refining and reducing dependence on imports from China. The contribution is conditional on due diligence and the successful negotiation of a final Contribution Agreement between NRCan and the recipient.

Ucore Rare Metals Inc is a Canadian advanced materials company specializing in rare earth element (REE) separation and refining technologies.

In this context, Tim Hodgson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, said: “Canada is moving quickly to secure the critical minerals that power our clean energy future, advanced manufacturing and national defence. Through the Critical Minerals Production Alliance and the G7 Critical Minerals Action Plan, we are mobilizing capital, forging international partnerships and using every tool at our disposal to build resilient, sustainable and secure supply chains. These investments are foundational to Canada’s sovereignty, competitiveness and leadership in the global economy".

interventions.title: Canada: Government announces CAD 14.1 million in state aid for Focus Graphite's graphite purification R&D under the G7 Critical Minerals Action Plan
interventions.description:
On 31 October 2025, Tim Hodgson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, announced that Focus Graphite Inc had received conditional approval for up to CAD 14.1 (USD 10.02) million in support from Natural Resources Canada’s Global Partnerships Initiative. The funding will support a chemical-free electrothermal purification R&D project for natural flake graphite, undertaken in collaboration with Ukraine’s Thermal & Material Engineering Center (TMEC) and the American Energy Technologies Company (AETC). The government did not specify the form the state aid would take.

According to the government, the project aims to demonstrate Canada’s first high-temperature electrothermal purification process for natural flake graphite sourced from the Lac Knife and Lac Tétépisca deposits—two of North America’s highest-grade graphite resources. This initiative forms part of a broader CAD 20.2 million package supporting collaborative research and development in critical minerals.

In this context, Minister Hodgson stated: “Canada is moving quickly to secure the critical minerals that power our clean energy future, advanced manufacturing and national defence. Through the Critical Minerals Production Alliance and the G7 Critical Minerals Action Plan, we are mobilizing capital, forging international partnerships and using every tool at our disposal to build resilient, sustainable and secure supply chains. These investments are foundational to Canada’s sovereignty, competitiveness and leadership in the global economy.”

interventions.title: Australia: AUD 600 million to support Glencore's copper smelter in Mount Isa and the refinery in Townsville
interventions.description:
On 8 October 2025, the Australian government announced the co-financing of an investment package of up to AUD 600 million (USD 385.5 million) over three years to support Glencore’s Mount Isa Copper Smelter and Townsville Refinery. The beneficiary is an Australian company engaged in the mining, smelting, and refining of metals and minerals. The type of support that will be provided was not specified.

The support will be granted alongside the Queensland government (see related intervention). According to the press release, the package also includes a transformation study to assess options for modernising the Mount Isa Smelter and Townsville Refinery, improving efficiency and environmental performance, and supporting future processing opportunities in North West Queensland. Furthermore, the measure targets operational stability and asset integrity projects to ensure the continued viability of these critical facilities and protect direct jobs at the nearby Phosphate Hill facility.

In this context, Tim Ayres, Minister for Industry, Innovation and Minister of Science, said: “Copper is critical to building solar panels, wind turbines and energy storage systems. This investment strengthens our supply chains and supports Australia’s transition to net zero".

interventions.title: Australia: AUD 600 million to support Glencore's copper smelter in Mount Isa and the refinery in Townsville
interventions.description:
On 8 October 2025, the Queensland government announced the co-financing of an investment package of up to AUD 600 million (USD 385.5 million) over three years to support Glencore’s Mount Isa Copper Smelter and Townsville Refinery. The beneficiary is an Australian company engaged in the mining, smelting, and refining of metals and minerals. The type of support that will be provided was not specified.

The support will be granted alongside the Australian national government (see related intervention). According to the press release, the package also includes a transformation study to assess options for modernising the Mount Isa Smelter and Townsville Refinery, improving efficiency and environmental performance, and supporting future processing opportunities in North West Queensland. Furthermore, the measure targets operational stability and asset integrity projects to ensure the continued viability of these critical facilities and protect direct jobs at the nearby Phosphate Hill facility.

In this context, Tim Ayres, Minister for Industry, Innovation and Minister of Science, said: “Copper is critical to building solar panels, wind turbines and energy storage systems. This investment strengthens our supply chains and supports Australia’s transition to net zero".

interventions.title: Argentina: RIGI benefits approved for "Proyecto Los Azules" project from Andes Corporación Minera SA
interventions.description:
On 14 October 2025, the Ministry of Economy of Argentina published Resolution No. 1553/2025 approving the "Proyecto Los Azules" as a beneficiary of the Incentive Regime for Large Investments (RIGI in Spanish). The project will be managed by Andes Corporación Minera SA, a company that explores for gold, silver, and copper in Argentina. Under RIGI, the project will obtain tax benefits for five years. The effective adhesion date is 25 September 2025, hence the tax benefits will apply retroactively.

The tax benefits include a 5-year cap on the Income Tax of 25% (the normal rate progressively increases until 35%), and tax credits applicable to the VAT. Additionally, Andes Corporación Minera SA will be able to import certain goods exempted from import duties and internal taxes (see related interventions).

The "Proyecto Los Azules" aims to complete the development (feasibility, permits) and construction of the mine, plant and associated infrastructure to extract and process copper cathodes. This project is located in the Andes Mountains region, Province of San Juan, Argentine Republic. According to the Resolution, the project will “encourage major domestic and foreign investments in the Argentine Republic in order to guarantee the country's prosperity”.

interventions.title: Indonesia: Government removed the export ban on copper, iron, lead, and zinc with certain requirements
interventions.description:
On 5 March 2025, the Minister of Energy and Mineral Resources of Indonesia issued Regulation No. 6/2025, establishing the export ban on copper, iron, lead, and zinc starting on 6 September 2025.

According to the regulation, the objective is to ensure the sustainability of national income from the minerals.

The export ban is established as the previous export permit subject to the smelter development regime will end on 5 September 2025.

interventions.title: India: Incentive scheme announced for recycling critical minerals
interventions.description:
On 3 September 2025, the Union Cabinet of India approved an Incentive Scheme with a budget of INR 1'500 crore (approx. USD 170.26 million) to develop recycling capacity for the separation and production of critical minerals from secondary sources. As part of the scheme, a 20% subsidy on capital expenditure incurred for starting production will be provided. The scheme will also provide support through a subsidy based on incremental sales over the base year (see related intervention). The scheme will be effective from the year 2025-2026 to 2030-2031*.

The scheme supports recycling of feedstock such as e-waste, lithium-ion battery scrap, and other kinds of scrap, for eg, catalytic converters of end-of-life vehicles. One-third of the scheme budget has been allocated for support to small and new recyclers.

The total support per entity has been capped at INR 50 crore for large entities and INR 25 crore for small entities, with the cap on sales incentives at INR 10 crore and INR 5 crore, respectively. The scheme is part of the National Critical Mineral Mission.

* Indian financial year ends on 31 March

interventions.title: India: Incentive scheme announced for recycling critical minerals
interventions.description:
On 3 September 2025, the Union Cabinet of India approved an Incentive Scheme with a budget of INR 1'500 crore (approx. USD 170.26 million) to develop recycling capacity for the separation and production of critical minerals from secondary sources. As part of the scheme, an incentive for the incremental sales each year over the base year will be provided. The scheme will also provide support through a subsidy on capital expenditure (see related intervention). The scheme will be effective from the year 2025-2026 to 2030-2031*.

The scheme supports recycling of feedstock such as e-waste, lithium-ion battery scrap, and other kinds of scrap, for eg, catalytic converters of end-of-life vehicles. One-third of the scheme budget has been allocated for support to small and new recyclers.

The total support per entity has been capped at INR 50 crore for large entities and INR 25 crore for small entities, with the cap on sales incentives at INR 10 crore and INR 5 crore, respectively. The scheme is part of the National Critical Mineral Mission.

* Indian financial year ends on 31 March

interventions.title: France: Government announced EUR 200 million in tax credits for Imerys' lithium project
interventions.description:
On 1 September 2025, the French Minister of Industry and Energy, Marc Ferracci, announced that the government had granted EUR 200 million (USD 234.6 million) in green industry tax credits to support Imerys’ EMILI lithium mining project. Imerys is a French company specialising in the production and transformation of industrial minerals.

The support is granted under the green industry tax credit (Crédit d’impôt au titre des investissements dans l’industrie verte – C3IV), introduced by the Loi Industrie Verte and implemented in the context of the France 2030 investment programme. The C3IV is an approval-based tax incentive: eligible projects must receive individual authorisation (“agrément”) from the authorities and are granted a project-specific tax credit envelope, rather than benefiting automatically from a general tax rule.

The tax credit supports the EMILI project led by Imerys, which aims to establish the first lithium mining operation in France, located in Echassières (Allier). According to the government, the associated refining facility is expected to produce up to 34'000 tonnes of lithium hydroxide per year by 2030.

In this context, Marc Ferracci stated (translated): “Relaunching mining activity in France means reconnecting with the obvious: there is no industry without resources, no energy transition without minerals. We are moving forward: the reform of the mining code secures the revival of exploration, Imerys obtains the green industry tax credit, and France 2030 relocates the production of critical metals to France.”

interventions.title: Australia: ARENA announces AUD 500 million Battery Breakthrough Initiative to support and grow domestic battery manufacturing
interventions.description:
On 19 August 2025, the Australian Renewable Energy Agency (ARENA) allocated AUD 500 million (USD 323 million) under the Battery Breakthrough Initiative (BBI) to support projects through capital grants. Funding for these projects will also be provided via production incentives (see related intervention).

According to the announcement, the funding will be used for three key areas of battery manufacturing:

  • Active materials (projects for cathode, anode and electrolyte production),
  • Battery cell manufacturing (projects supporting manufacture of battery cells and components)
  • Battery pack assembly (projects related to connecting cells and modules into battery packs).

The programme is also intended to provide clean energy manufacturing opportunities for Australia’s workforce and allow the country to leverage its expertise in battery energy storage.

In this context, ARENA CEO Darren Miller said: “Energy storage is a critical enabling technology for our renewable energy future. We know that solar and wind energy can provide us the lowest cost renewable energy generation at scale, but we also need to be able to store it for when it is needed.”

“BBI will enhance Australia’s battery manufacturing capability, improve supply chain resilience and battery manufacturing processes, and foster innovation.”

Notably, ARENA was launched in 2011 and counted with a total budget of AUD 2.5 billion (USD 1.82 billion) that were to be used until 30 June 2020. The institution received additional funding in 2020 (please see related state act). The new budget allocated to ARENA has extended the existence and budget of this institution, providing it with a total budget of AUD 6.02 billion (over USD 4.3 billion) to finance projects related to renewable energy and energy efficiency.

The Australian Renewable Energy Agency (ARENA)

ARENA is a publicly financed corporation which, among other duties, provides, funding for research in clean energy, the development, deployment and commercialization of renewable energy, and the storage and dissemination of information related to clean energy.
