interventions.title: Germany: Government granted EUR 103.6 million in financial support to Vulcan Energy for lithium extraction and processing projects
interventions.description:
On 22 July 2025, the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE) announced a financial contribution of EUR 41.44 million (USD 49 million), corresponding to 40% of the total public funding of EUR 103.6 million, to support two investment projects for lithium extraction and processing in Germany. These projects are co-financed with the states of Rhineland-Palatinate and Hesse, each contributing 30% of the total amount (see related interventions). The type of financial support that will be provided was not specified.

According to the federal government, the funding supports the combined development of lithium extraction in Landau and the further processing of lithium chloride into lithium hydroxide monohydrate in Frankfurt-Höchst. The projects are led by subsidiaries of Vulcan Energy Resources GmbH, namely Natürlich Landau Lithium GmbH and Vulcan Projektgesellschaft 2 GmbH, operating in Landau and Frankfurt-Höchst, respectively.

The processed product, lithium hydroxide monohydrate, is described as a key starting material for modern lithium-ion batteries and supports electromobility and energy storage applications.

interventions.title: Germany: Government granted EUR 103.6 million in financial support to Vulcan Energy for lithium extraction and processing projects
interventions.description:
On 22 July 2025, the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE) announced that the government of Hesse would contribute EUR 31.08 million (USD 36.4 million), representing 30% of the total public funding of EUR 103.6 million, to support a lithium processing project within its territory. The type of financial support that will be provided was not specified.

According to the project description, the funding supports the construction of a conversion plant in Frankfurt-Höchst by Vulcan Projektgesellschaft 2 GmbH, a subsidiary of Vulcan Energy Resources GmbH. This plant will further process the lithium chloride produced in Landau into lithium hydroxide monohydrate.

interventions.title: Germany and Bulgaria: EIB and Aurubis AG signed an investment loan for driving recycling and copper production
interventions.description:
On 17 July 2025, the European Investment Bank (EIB) and Aurubis AG signed a EUR 200 million (USD 232 million) loan. The loan will finance strategic projects to expand copper refining at its Bulgarian site and scale up metal recycling and environmental protection at its Hamburg plant. Aurubis AG is a German supplier of non-ferrous metals and a copper recycler.

According to the press release, this five-year loan reinforces sustainable metal production and supports European Union policy objectives such as economic growth, regional development, and sustainable business practices. It represents the first EIB financing for the sector since the adoption of the new EIB Group strategy to secure Europe’s access to critical raw materials, actively supporting the rollout of the Critical Raw Materials Act.

In this context, Nicola Beer, Vice President of the European Investment Bank, noted: “Securing critical raw materials like copper and expanding recycling activities in Europe are essential to Europe’s resilience, sustainable development, and industrial competitiveness. Copper is the hidden backbone of our modern lives — it keeps the lights on in our homes and powers the smartphone in our pockets, while also driving innovation in electric cars and renewable energy.”

The EIB is the EU's main lending bank, owned by the EU member states. The EIB provides long-term financing for projects that contribute to EU policy objectives, including climate action, digitalisation, infrastructure development, and support for mid-caps and SMEs.

interventions.title: Canada: CIB provides a CAD 55 million loan to Torngat Metals' rare earths project
interventions.description:
On 23 June 2025, Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) provided a CAD 55 million (approx. USD 40.12 million) loan to Torngat Metals. Torngat Metals is developing the Strange Lake rare earth minerals project in Nunavik and Sept-Îles.

The loan supports the next phase of the project, enabling essential infrastructure work such as utility connections, airstrip rehabilitation, and improvements to existing facilities to prepare for the future development of an open-pit mine and concentration plant. This financing also aims to bridge project financing gaps and mitigate risks associated with early-stage mining development in Canada's critical minerals sector.

In this context, Ehren Cory, CEO of Canada Infrastructure Bank, said: "The CIB is pleased to provide a project finance loan to Torngat Metals to advance the next phase of the Strange Lake mining project - our first in the critical minerals sector. We are proud to partner with the private sector to catalyze investment in rare earths and advance nationally significant projects that strengthen Canada’s position in global exports markets."

Canada Infrastructure Bank

Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) is a federal financial agency investing in revenue-generating infrastructure across Canada. The CIB partners with private, public, and institutional investors to support its projects. It employs multiple funding tools including equity, loans, and derivatives.

interventions.title: Morocco: Government imposes temporary export licensing requirement on certain copper and aluminum alloys
interventions.description:
On 24 April 2025, the Moroccan government imposed a temporary export licensing requirement on certain copper and aluminum alloys. The decision was announced through Ministerial Decision No. 693 of 2025 in the Official Gazette No.7398. It enters into force on the day of publication and is in place for 24 months.

The affected products are classified under HS codes subheadings 7403.19, 7403.22, 7403.29 for certain copper alloys and 7601.10 and 7601.20 for certain aluminum alloys.

interventions.title: China: Government announces export control measures for various items related to rare earths
interventions.description:
On 4 April 2025, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce adopted Announcement 2025/18, announcing export control measures on various items related to certain medium and heavy rare earths. The measure is implemented from the day of publication. 

Specifically, the announcement covers items related to samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, and yttrium. The announcement specified 33 HS codes at the 10-digit level. 

For all items in the announcement, exporters need to apply for special export licenses and fill in documentation for dual-use items and technologies. The Ministry of Commerce takes a decision based on the application documents. Exporting without a licence results in penalties and a possible further investigation. 

The stated objection is to "safeguard national security and interests and fulfill international obligations such as non-proliferation".

On the same day, the Chinese government adopted additional measures, including additional tariffs on imports from the US and additions of US companies to its Unreliable Entity List and its Export Control List (see related state acts).

interventions.title: South Africa: Industrial Development Corporation provides a ZAR 800 million loan to Palabora Copper (Pty) Ltd
interventions.description:
In March 2025, the Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) of South Africa disclosed it had provided a general loan of ZAR 800 million (USD 42.72 million) to Palabora Copper (Pty) Ltd. The beneficiary is a copper mining and processing company. The company produces refined copper products such as electrolytic tough pitch (ETP) copper rod, as well as by-products from copper refining including anode slimes (rich in precious and rare metals), sulphuric acid, magnetite, vermiculite, and nickel sulphate crystals.

The loan was disclosed in the IDC-Funded Business Partner List for the financial year 2024/25, which in South Africa ends on 31 March 2025. In the absence of a specified date for the loan, the end of FY 2025 was selected as an inception date.

Industrial Development Corporation (IDC)

The Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC) is a state-owned development finance institution that provides funding and support to grow industrial capacity, promote economic diversification, and drive job creation in South Africa and the wider region.

interventions.title: South Africa: SARS introduces export permit requirements for certain copper products
interventions.description:
On 7 March 2025, the South African Revenue Service mandated export permits for certain tariff headings related to copper products. This requirement applies to 7403.12, 7403.13, 7403.19, 7403.21, 7403.22, and 7403.29.

Export permits can be granted by the International Trade Administration Commission of South Africa (ITAC).

interventions.title: Canada: Authorities allocated funding worth CAD 20 million towards Critical Elements Lithium Corporation
interventions.description:
On 6 February 2025, the Canadian government provided state aid worth CAD 20 million (approx. USD 14 million) to Critical Elements Lithium Corporation. The funds are expected to assist the firm's construction of an electrical station to service its lithium-tantalum mining project in Quebec. The state aid is provided under the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF) and is announced pending final due diligence. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. 

Five other firms also received funding, however, these did not meet the GTA reporting criteria.

In this context, the Honourable Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources said: " These projects, under the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy, will help expand Quebec’s sustainable critical minerals production, notably rare earths that are used in electronics, clean energy, aerospace, automotive and defence. Developments like this help mines get built faster, and they are a key element in seizing the generational opportunity before us. The Government of Canada is supporting projects that strengthen Canada’s supply chains, enhance our ability to be a reliable supplier of the critical minerals the world is demanding and foster economic growth while creating good jobs".

Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund 

The Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF) is a Canadian federal initiative providing up to $1.5 billion in funding over seven years (2023–2030) to support clean energy and transportation infrastructure projects that enable the sustainable development and expansion of critical minerals. The CMIF offers two avenues for support: grants for Indigenous engagement and capacity building and contribution funding for preconstruction activities and shovel-ready infrastructure projects.

interventions.title: Canada: Government announces CAD 41 million in state aid for the Foran Mining Corporation copper project
interventions.description:
On 28 January 2025, the government of Canada announced CAD 41 (USD 28.47) million in state aid for the Foran Mining Corporation sustainable copper project in the McIlvenna Bay mine. The government will provide the funding through the Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF). 

This state aid will enable the acquisition of electric vehicles, installation of an advanced ventilation system with heat recovery capabilities, development of water conservation infrastructure, and implementation of a pyrite extraction system. According to the government press release, Foran Mining aims to produce carbon-neutral copper at the mine. 

In December 2024, the government announced CAD 20 million in funding for this project via the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (see related state acts). The government notes that the investment is part of the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy. 

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, said: “Developing our abundant critical minerals resources in Saskatchewan will drive economic opportunity and create jobs. Foran’s McIlvenna Bay mining project alone will support nearly 400 jobs and reinforce Saskatchewan’s position as a global mining leader by producing more Canadian copper and zinc to supply our own needs and those of our allies. Canada will continue to be a partner in the development of our critical minerals supply chains, from end to end, in order to drive economic benefits and contribute to a secure future". 

Strategic Innovation Fund 

Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF) is a government funding program established to make Canada a hub for businesses to invest and grow. The SIF targets industrial and technology sectors and provides funding to large-scale (over CAD 10 million in requested contribution) projects. The SIF's objectives are boosting Canadian businesses' competitiveness, promoting clean growth and the country's technological edge. Government contributions under SIF are generally repayable but in exceptional cases come through financial grants.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE provides USD 996 million loan guarantee to Ioneer Rhyolite Ridge LLC for lithium carbonate development
interventions.description:
On 17 January 2025, the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced the approval of a USD 996 million loan guarantee to Ioneer Rhyolite Ridge LLC. The funding aims to support the development of a domestic supply of lithium carbonate for electric vehicle (EV) batteries as part of the Rhyolite Ridge Lithium-Boron Project in Esmeralda County, Nevada.

According to the DOE, the loan will finance the on-site processing of lithium carbonate, which is anticipated to contribute to the production of lithium for over 370'000 EVs annually. The DOE suggests that, if successful, the project could potentially reduce gasoline consumption by approximately 176 million gallons per year and prevent the release of an estimated 1.56 million tons of carbon dioxide annually. The application for this loan was originally submitted by Ioneer in October 2021.

The project's funding was announced through the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program, established under the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007. The program provides low-interest loans to support the development and manufacturing of fuel-efficient vehicles and advanced vehicle technologies in the U.S.

interventions.title: United States of America: Approval of funding for semiconductor-grade polysilicon production
interventions.description:
On 7 January 2025, the U.S. Department of Commerce announced an award of up to USD 320 million in grants to Hemlock Semiconductor (HSC) under the CHIPS Incentives Program. The funding supports the domestic production of semiconductor-grade polysilicon, a material critical for manufacturing advanced semiconductors. Hemlock Semiconductor produces polysilicon and silicon-based materials at its manufacturing facility in Hemlock, Michigan.

According to the announcement, HSC will use the funding to expand its production facilities in Saginaw County, Michigan, with construction expected to begin in 2026 and production starting in 2028. The expansion is projected to create approximately 180 manufacturing jobs and additional construction jobs. This initiative is part of broader efforts to strengthen the U.S. semiconductor supply chain under the CHIPS and Science Act.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo said: “CHIPS for America's investment in HSC will help advance supply chain security by ensuring the U.S. has a reliable, domestic supply of polysilicon – the bedrock of semiconductors. Establishing a domestic source of these materials is important for development of leading-edge chip applications, which helps bolster our economic and national security. Through targeted investments like this, the Biden-Harris Administration is driving technological innovation in industries of the future and creating jobs across the country.”

The government also allocated USD 5 million to the project through "CHIPS Workforce Funding." This state aid did not pass the GTA reporting criteria.

Funding was announced through the CHIPS and Science Act of 2022. The announcement of the funding opportunity for the present funding was made in February 2023 (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: US Administration announces tariff increases under Section 301 on China for tungsten products, wafers, and polysilicon
interventions.description:
On 11 December 2024, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) released a notice detailing modifications following the statutory four-year review of tariffs imposed on certain Chinese-origin products under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974. Specifically, the USTR increased tariff rates for solar wafers and polysilicon from 25% to 50% and imposed new tariffs on tungsten products at a rate of 25%. The affected products are classified under tariff subheadings 8101.94, 8101.99, 2804.61, and 3818.00. These tariff increases will take effect on 1 January 2025.

The tariff increases are the culmination of a statutory four-year review of Section 301 tariffs initially imposed following a 2017 investigation into China’s unfair trade practices, including forced technology transfer, intellectual property theft, and industrial espionage (see related state act). Previously, in September 2024, the USTR introduced a wide range of tariff increases targeting critical sectors, such as electric vehicles, semiconductors, solar cells, and critical minerals (see related state act).

In this context, U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai said: “The tariff increases announced today will further blunt the harmful policies and practices by the People’s Republic of China. These actions will complement the domestic investments made under the Biden-Harris Administration to promote a clean energy economy, while increasing the resilience of critical supply chains.”

interventions.title: Canada: Government announces CAD 20 million in state aid to Foran Mining Corporation to support copper production
interventions.description:
On 20 December 2024, the government of Canada, through the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund, conditionally approved state aid of up to CAD 20 (USD 14) million for Foran Mining Corporation's McIlvenna Bay copper mine project in Saskatchewan. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. Foran Mining Corporation is a Canadian mining company focused on developing carbon-neutral copper and zinc projects in Saskatchewan, with their flagship McIlvenna Bay deposit in the Flin Flon Greenstone Belt.

The government funding is conditional upon the completion of due diligence. 

The funding will support the construction of a hydro transmission line, an on-site substation, and electric vehicle charging infrastructure, aiming to fully electrify the mine and achieve carbon-neutral copper production. According to the government, the action is in line with Canada's Critical Minerals Strategy, which seeks to decarbonize industrial mining operations, strengthen supply chains, and advance economic reconciliation by involving Indigenous Peoples in infrastructure and critical minerals projects.

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, said: “Developing Canada’s abundant critical minerals drives economic opportunity and creates good jobs. In its drive to produce copper without polluting more, Foran’s project will help reinforce Canada’s position as a global supplier of choice for clean technology, clean energy and the resources the world needs to build a prosperous economy. Foran and many other businesses in Saskatchewan and beyond know that, moving forward, economic development and environmental sustainability must go hand in hand".

interventions.title: Japan: Government announces JPY 23.9 billion to support several storage battery projects
interventions.description:
On 20 December 2024, the government of Japan announced JPY 6.9 billion (USD 44.2 million) in financial grants to support Mitsubishi Chemical Corporation's storage battery development plan. According to the plan, the company will produce natural graphite-based anode material.

Mitsubishi Chemical Corporation is a Japanese multinational company specializing in the development of chemical and material solutions, including performance polymers, advanced materials, and sustainable chemical products for industries such as electronics, automotive, and healthcare.

The government also announced support for other companies' battery production plans (see related interventions) under the government's Economic Security Promotion Act. Some interventions did not meet the GTA reporting criteria. 

The measure's stated objective is to ensure a stable supply of storage batteries.

interventions.title: Japan: Government announces a JPY 13.2 billion financial grant to Hyuga Smelting Co Ltd for producing nickel matte
interventions.description:
On 13 December 2024, the government of Japan announced approximately JPY 13.2 billion (USD 85.85 million) in support of the critical minerals supply security plan of Hyuga Smelting Co Ltd. The aid will take the form of a financial grant.

According to the plan, "by installing a new converter, they will start producing nickel matte using ferronickel as the main raw material."

Hyuga Smelting Co Ltd is a Japanese company specialising in the smelting and refining of non-ferrous metals and producing high-quality metal products for various industries.

The measure's stated objective is to ensure a stable supply of nickel matte, which is a critical mineral. The support is granted under Japan's Economic Security Promotion Act.

interventions.title: Australia: Export Finance Australia announces an AUD 475 million financing towards the Eneabba Rare Earths Refinery project
interventions.description:
On 6 December 2024, the Export Finance Australia (EFA) allocated AUD 475 million (approx. USD 303.2 million) funding in the form of a state loan to Iluka Resources Limited for the Eneabba Rare Earths Refinery project. The EFA funding has been channelled through the Australian government’s AUD 4 billion Critical Minerals Facility (please see related state act).

The Eneabba Rare Earths Refinery will produce rare earth oxide products such as praseodymium, dysprosium, neodymium and terbium.

The Australian government previously allocated funds towards the Eneabba Rare Earths Refinery project (please see related state act).

In this context, Minister for Trade and Tourism, Don Farrell stated: "Our Government is committed to becoming a renewable energy superpower, not just to tackle the global challenge of climate change but also to harness the opportunity to create significant high paying, secure jobs for Australians. Our country is blessed with an abundance of critical minerals and the Albanese Labor Government wants Australians to benefit from this emerging sector".

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Energy announces a USD 475 million loan for a lithium battery recycling plant
interventions.description:
In November 2024, the US Department of Energy (DOE) announced providing USD 475 million in loans to Li-Cycle US Holdings Inc for the construction of a lithium-ion battery resource recovery facility in New York.

When completed the facility will collect and process discarded lithium batteries and it will recover critical EV battery materials. This facility will support the battery needs of over 200,000 electric vehicles annually.

According to the announcement from DOE: "The critical materials that make up EV batteries are in high demand and can be difficult to procure. The Li-Cycle project will use a technique called hydrometallurgical recycling to efficiently recover battery-grade lithium carbonate, cobalt sulfate, nickel sulfate, and other critical materials from manufacturing scrap materials and used batteries. Li-Cycle’s hydrometallurgical process will recover these critical battery materials with high efficiency and with low energy use, operational costs, and emissions. The final product will be a U.S.-made supply of battery-grade critical materials."

Funding is provided through the DOE’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) program. Authorized by the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, ATVM supports the manufacturing of eligible vehicles and qualifying components. Given that LPO did not specify the exact approval date of the award, GTA follows a conservative approach selecting 31 November 2024 as the inception date.

interventions.title: Australia (Western Australian): Authorities announce AUD 140 million in funding and incentives to support the lithium sector
interventions.description:
On 27 November 2024, the Western Australian government announced incentives in the form of a waiver on government fees up to AUD 90 million (approx. USD 58.4 million) to support downstream lithium processing through the Lithium Industry Support Program. The measure is temporary and will be in place for two years.

The Australian government also announced an AUD 50 million loan facility to support lithium miners (please see related intervention).

In this context, Premier Roger Cook stated: "This package will provide important temporary and responsible support for WA's fledgling lithium industry, taking into account the extremely challenging market conditions it is facing. This package is aimed at helping our critical lithium industry and its workforce to the other side of this turbulent period - supporting local jobs and doing what's right for WA".

interventions.title: Australia (Western Australian): Authorities announce AUD 140 million in funding and incentives to support the lithium sector
interventions.description:
On 27 November 2024, the Western Australian government announced an AUD 50 million (approx. USD 32.5 million) temporary interest-free loan scheme to support lithium miners and help them sustain their operations.

The loans will be interest-free until the average lithium spodumene prices exceed USD 1'100 per tonne for two quarters or by 30 June 2026, whichever is earlier.  The companies will be required to repay the loans within two years after the end of the interest-free period.

The West Australian government also announced a temporary waiver on government fees for lithium mining firms (please see related intervention). Finally, the West Australian government also announced a waiver on port charges and mining tenement fees, however, this did not meet the GTA reporting criteria. 

In this context, Premier Roger Cook stated: "This package will provide important temporary and responsible support for WA's fledgling lithium industry, taking into account the extremely challenging market conditions it is facing. This package is aimed at helping our critical lithium industry and its workforce to the other side of this turbulent period - supporting local jobs and doing what's right for WA".

interventions.title: Canada (Quebec): Government announced CAD 250 million in state aid to Nemaska ​​Lithium
interventions.description:
On 6 November 2024, the government of Quebec announced CAD 250 million (USD 178.89 million) in state aid to Nemaska Lithium to support a lithium hydroxide project. The funding will provided through Investissement Quebec, the province's development financial institution. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. 

In this context, Christine Fréchette, Minister of Economy, Innovation and Energy and Minister responsible for Regional Economic Development, said: "We are investing $250 million to carry out regional economic development projects and the transformation of critical and strategic minerals here in Quebec. With the battery sector, we are consolidating our nation's place as a leader in the energy transition, in addition to creating paying jobs for Quebecers. We are continuing the work to develop a strong battery sector that will enrich and decarbonize Quebec!".

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Energy announces a USD 2.3 billion loan to Lithium Nevada Corp for the construction of a lithium carbonate processing plant
interventions.description:
On 28 October 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced a loan for Lithium Americas Corp’s subsidiary, Lithium Nevada Corp. The aid will support the construction of a lithium carbonate processing plant in Nevada. The total value of the funding is USD 2.26 billion.

According to the announcement from LPO, Lithium Nevada will build a lithium carbonate processing plant at Thacker Pass in Humboldt County, Nevada. The facility is planned to produce around 40’000 metric tonnes per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate for use in lithium-ion batteries. This is equivalent to batteries for over 800'000 electric vehicles (EVs) annually.

In this context, Jonathan Evans, President and Chief Executive Officer of Lithium Americas said: "The United States has an incredible opportunity to lead the next chapter of global electrification in a way that both strengthens our battery supply chains and ensures that the economic benefits are directed toward American workers, companies and communities."

Funding is announced through the DOE’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) program. Authorized by the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, ATVM supports the manufacturing of eligible vehicles and qualifying components and materials that improve fuel economy. A conditional loan commitment is an offer approved by the DOE to extend a loan to a project, contingent upon the project meeting specified technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before the DOE finalizes the financing agreement and disburses the funds. A conditional loan commitment is offered following the completion of the due diligence process.

On 30 September 025, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced that its Loan Programs Office had restructured its 2024 loan agreement with Lithium Americas Corp (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America (State of Michigan): Approval of funding for semiconductor-grade polysilicon production
interventions.description:
On 21 October 2024, the Michigan Economic Development Corporation announced financial support for Hemlock Semiconductor to support its semiconductor-grade polysilicon production. The total value of funding is USD 40 million and it will be provided in the form of grants.

According to the announcement, this funding is in addition to the proposed USD 325 million in federal support from the Commerce Department under the CHIPS and Science Act. Hemlock Semiconductor, one of the world’s largest producers of hyper-pure polysilicon and the only one headquartered in the United States, plans to expand its production capacity of semiconductor-grade polysilicon to meet the demands of leading-edge chip applications.

In this context, Governor Gretchen Whitmer said: “Today, cutting-edge Michigan company Hemlock Semiconductor secured a proposed $325 million investment from the Biden-Harris administration to create 180 good-paying jobs and build a new facility to help make polysilicon, a critical material that’s found in nearly every electronic device in the world. HSC is one of many Michigan-based companies that is helping us lead the future of advanced manufacturing. Thanks to the Biden-Harris administration’s bipartisan CHIPS and Science Act and the U.S. Department of Commerce, we are ensuring HSC’s global leadership in the powerful domestic supply chain to manufacture semiconductor chips needed for everything from computers and phones to AI applications. Together, we are strengthening national and economic security by ensuring supply chains, good-paying jobs, and cutting-edge investments are where they belong—here at home. Let’s build the future we all want—one where America leads.”

Funding was announced through the ‘Make it in Michigan Competitiveness Fund’. Launched in May 2023, the Fund was designed to enhance Michigan's economic growth by securing federal investments from legislations such as the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law, the CHIPS and Science Act, and the Inflation Reduction Act. The fund provides matching grants to projects that require non-federal contributions, aiming to unlock federally backed investments in target sectors (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces USD 150 million in funding for ABTC to support the production of battery materials
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award American Battery Technology Company (ABTC) USD 150 million in funding to support the construction of a commercial-scale lithium-ion battery recycling facility in South Carolina. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, ABTC will process approximately 100'000 tonnes of battery materials annually from its battery manufacturer and automotive OEM partners, producing battery-grade minerals such as nickel, cobalt, manganese, copper, aluminium, graphite, and lithium hydroxide for resale.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. The funding opportunity (FOA) of this program was issued in November 2023 (see related state act) and represents the second FOA under the program; the first FOA was announced in May 2022 (see related state act) with awardees selected in October 2022. Including this award, DOE announced approximately USD 3 billion for 25 projects across 14 states to increase the domestic production of advanced batteries and battery materials.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “We’re in the midst of a manufacturing revival in the United States as the Biden-Harris Administration’s Investing in America agenda continues to breathe new life into communities and local economies across the country. By positioning the U.S. at the forefront of advanced battery manufacturing, we are creating high-paying jobs and strengthening our global economic leadership and domestic energy security, all while supporting the clean energy transition.”

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards Ascend Elements Inc USD 125 million to support the company’s graphite recovery project
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award Ascend Elements Inc USD 125 million in funding to construct facilities in Kentucky to recover and purify graphite from recycled lithium-ion batteries into battery-grade graphite. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, the project will build facilities in Hopkinsville, KY, to convert graphite residue from battery recycling into high-purity (>99.95%) battery-grade graphite with a lower carbon footprint. This will strengthen the domestic supply chain for anode materials and support a circular economy by recovering critical minerals from recycling streams.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards Blue Whale Materials LLC USD 55.2 million to expand lithium-ion battery recycling facility
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award Blue Whale Materials LLC USD 55.2 million in funding to expand a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Bartlesville, OK, increasing capacity to process end-of-life batteries and produce mixed metal precursor. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, the project will enlarge Blue Whale Materials' existing recycling facility to process up to 50'000 tonnes per year of lithium-ion batteries. It will produce high-purity mixed metal precursor containing cobalt, nickel, and lithium for reuse in battery production, supporting the domestic supply of critical materials.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.description:

Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, Cirba Solutions will construct and operate a facility capable of processing over 60'000 tons of lithium-ion batteries annually. It will recover critical materials to produce battery-grade salts for cathode manufacturing, increasing domestic supply and reducing reliance on foreign sources.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. According to the announcement, Clarios Circular Solutions will retrofit a facility to process battery production scrap into cathode active materials at a rate of 20'000 tonnes per year. This supports domestic battery production and reduces dependence on foreign processing.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards Element 25 (Louisiana) LLC USD 166.1 million to support the company’s manganese sulfate production project
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award Element 25 (Louisiana) LLC USD 166.1 million in funding to construct a facility near Baton Rouge, LA, to produce high-purity manganese sulfate monohydrate from manganese ore. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, Element 25 will construct a facility near Baton Rouge, LA, to produce approximately 65'000 metric tonnes per year of high-purity manganese sulfate monohydrate from manganese ore. This supports domestic battery manufacturing and reduces dependency on foreign sources.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards Revex Technologies Inc USD 145 million to support the company’s critical minerals processing facilities project
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award Revex Technologies Inc USD 145 million in funding to construct facilities in Champion and Gwinn, MI, that will process mine tailings and spent batteries to produce critical minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and lithium. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, Revex Technologies, in partnership with Eagle Mine, will build and operate three facilities in Champion and Gwinn, MI, to process mine tailings and spent batteries. The facilities will produce approximately 21'250 tonnes per year of nickel concentrate and 16'000 tonnes per year of black mass containing cobalt, nickel, and lithium, supporting domestic production of critical minerals for electric vehicle batteries.  In addition to the USD 145 million in federal funding, the project is funded by up to USD 100 million in funding from the State of Michigan, contingent upon receiving federal support.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards SWA Lithium LLC USD 225 million to support the company’s lithium carbonate production project
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award SWA Lithium LLC USD 225 million in funding to produce battery-quality lithium carbonate in Lewisville, AR, using direct lithium extraction from brine. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, SWA Lithium will develop a facility in southern Arkansas to extract lithium from brine in the Smackover Formation using direct lithium extraction technology. The project aims to produce up to 45'000 tonnes per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate over a 20-year operating life, establishing a significant domestic source of lithium.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.title: United States of America: DOE awards TerraVolta Resources USD 225 million to support the company’s lithium extraction and refining project
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award TerraVolta Resources USD 225 million in funding to build a commercial-scale facility in the Texarkana region to extract and refine lithium from brine using direct lithium extraction. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, TerraVolta Resources will design, build, and operate a facility in the Texarkana region to produce battery-grade lithium carbonate from domestic brine resources using direct lithium extraction technology. The facility will have an annual production capacity of 25'000 tonnes of lithium carbonate equivalent, including refining capabilities to convert lithium chloride into lithium carbonate.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces USD 67 million in funding for Albemarle US Inc to support the production of anode material for lithium-ion batteries
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award Albemarle U.S. Inc USD 67.1 million in funding to support the production of lithium metal anode (LMA) for next-generation lithium-ion batteries. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, Albemarle will retrofit a domestic manufacturing facility in Charlotte, North Carolina to produce commercial quantities of anode material for next-generation lithium-ion batteries. The facility will make up to 50 metric tons per annum of LMA for downstream advanced battery cell manufacturing facilities.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. The funding opportunity (FOA) of this program was issued in November 2023 (see related state act) and represents the second FOA under the program; the first FOA was announced in May 2022 (see related state act) with awardees selected in October 2022. Granholm said: “We’re in the midst of a manufacturing revival in the United States as the Biden-Harris Administration’s Investing in America agenda continues to breathe new life into communities and local economies across the country. By positioning the U.S. at the forefront of advanced battery manufacturing, we are creating high-paying jobs and strengthening our global economic leadership and domestic energy security, all while supporting the clean energy transition.”

interventions.title: Canada: Government provides CAD 20 million for copper mine development in British Columbia
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, Canada's Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, Jonathan Wilkinson, announced a CAD 20 (USD 13.81) million contribution through the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF) for the Galore Creek Mining Corporation's copper mine development in the Tahltan Territory, British Columbia.

Scheduled to commence on 1 September 2024, the project will construct a 43-kilometre access road connecting the mine to existing infrastructure, a necessary step to enhance copper supply. Pending Natural Resources Canada's final due diligence, the funding aims to facilitate access to mill and processing facilities and secure electricity via BC Hydro's low-emission grid, supporting partnerships with local First Nations in boosting regional critical minerals development. 

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson said: “These two projects, under the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy’s flagship program, will develop the necessary infrastructure to access and transport our rich critical mineral resources in northern B.C. and the Yukon. Developments like these help mines get built faster, and they are a key element in seizing the generational opportunity before us. These investments are needed to support critical minerals development in the region, improve community access and safety, and create good mining jobs across British Columbia and the Yukon".

interventions.title: SACU: Customs duty increase on sulphates, alums, peroxosulphates (persulphates) of copper
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the South African Revenue Service (SARS) increased the rate of customs duty on sulphates; alums; peroxosulphates (persulphates) of copper from 0% to 10%. The product in question is classified under HS code 2833.25. The import duty increase does not apply to the EU/UK, EFTA, SADC, or AfCFTA countries. 

This measure resulted from an investigation by the International Trade Administration Commission (ITAC) whose findings were published in its Report No. 675.

interventions.title: Russia: Government allocates RUB 4 billion for the construction of the energy infrastructure for the new mineral processing plant
interventions.description:
On 19 September 2024, the government of Russia allocated RUB 4 billion (USD 42.2 million) to support the construction of the energy infrastructure for the new mineral processing plant at the Ak-Sug copper-porphyry deposit in the Republic of Tyva. According to the government, the new plant will be constructed by an unspecified private investor. 

Specifically, the government will fund the construction of overhead power lines and other energy infrastructure facilities needed to ensure the work of the enterprise. According to the press release, the government is financing the construction of this energy infrastructure to support this particular investment project. 

In this context, the Head of the Russian government Mikhail Mishustin said [translation]: "The government will allocate 4 billion rubles to implement investment projects for the extraction and processing of non-ferrous metals in the Republic of Tyva, which has significant raw material reserves. This will increase the attractiveness of this sector for business, help develop large deposits, support the construction of new mining and processing plants, and give additional impetus to the development of the entire region."

interventions.title: Japan: Government announces a JPY 1.5 billion financial grant to Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd to test the recycling of critical minerals
interventions.description:
On 10 September 2024, the government of Japan announced approximately JPY 1.5 billion (USD 10.54 million) in support of the critical minerals supply security plan of Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd. The aid will take the form of a financial grant.

According to the plan, "demonstration will be carried out at a pilot plant to recover and refine nickel, cobalt, and lithium from black mass produced in the lithium-ion battery recycling process."

Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd is a Japanese company specialising in the production and development of chemical products, including synthetic resins, adhesives, and functional materials for various industrial applications.

The measure's stated objective is to ensure a stable supply of critical minerals. The support is granted under Japan's Economic Security Promotion Act.

interventions.title: Finland: Finnvera signs EUR 250 million loan guarantee for Keliber Technology Oy's lithium project
interventions.description:
On 26 August 2024, the Finnish export credit agency Finnvera signed a EUR 250 million (USD 279.4 million) loan guarantee with Keliber Technology Oy. The guarantee will support the development of Keliber's lithium project in Kaustinen and Kokkola, Finland.

Keliber Technology Oy is a Finish mining and chemical company that produces battery-grade lithium hydroxide for electrification and energy storage. 

According to the press release, this type of instrument is granted to a "Finnish domestic investment that promotes exports. The Finnish interest in the Keliber project is strong, the procurements are almost entirely of domestic origin and the Finnish company Metso supplies the key technology of the project."

interventions.title: Finland: EIB provides financing for 'Keliber Battery Grade Lithium Production'
interventions.description:
On 20 August 2024, the European Investment Bank (EIB) and Keliber Oy signed a state loan agreement worth EUR 270 million (approx. USD 297 million) for the 'Keliber Battery Grade Lithium Production' project from Finland. The total cost of the project is estimated at EUR 723 million.

The EIB issued the following project description: 'Mining and production of battery grade lithium (Li) hydroxide. The project comprises the successive development and mining of several Li-mineral ore mines, a concentrator plant to produce an ore concentrate and an innovative first-of-a-kind hydro-metallurgical processing plant to produce battery grade lithium hydroxide (LiOH) with a nominal capacity of 15 kt/a'.

The objective of the project is defined as follows: 'The product is intended as a high-grade lithium hydroxide mainly for the Li-ion battery cell manufacturing industry in Europe'.

A state act in the GTA database is assessed solely in terms of the extent to which its implementation affects foreign commercial interests. On this metric, the investment support granted here is discriminatory.

interventions.title: Turkiye: Ministry of Industry and Technology announces a funding call worth USD 4.5 billion to support the production of battery cells and active materials
interventions.description:
On 26 July 2024, the Turkish Ministry of Industry and Technology announced a tax relief measure to support businesses investing in the production of battery cells and active materials. In addition to the tax relief, the Turkish government has announced a comprehensive package of incentives, including free land allocation, financial grants, state loans, and equity support (see related interventions). These combined incentives will be supported by a total budget of USD 4.5 billion.

According to the announcement from the Ministry, this funding call aims to increase Türkiye's production capacity to meet domestic demand and capitalize on the export potential, particularly to the European market. The goal is to establish a total production capacity of 80 GWh, focusing on local content and including investments in R&D centres. The call will support investments in battery and active material production facilities with a minimum annual capacity of 5 GWh, starting from the cell level. The tax relief for each company can be up to 80% of its investment value.

Funding was announced as part of the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, a government initiative aimed at attracting high-value-added investments in key technology sectors. This funding call is one of six announced by the Ministry for different priority sectors (see related state acts). It is stated that more funding calls will be released under the HIT-30 program.

In this context, Minister of Industry and Technology Mehmet Fatih Kacır said: “Under the HIT-30 High Technology Incentive Program, we aim to attract global investments to Turkey, especially in the fields of innovative emerging technologies. Through the first six calls, we intend to make Turkey a production hub for next-generation energy vehicles, battery technologies, chip technologies, solar cells, and wind turbines. Through these six calls, we will attract more than USD 20 billion in private sector investments to our country by 2030" (author's own translation).

In addition to the free land allocation, the Turkish government has announced a comprehensive package of incentives, including financial grants, tax reliefs, state loans, and equity support (see related interventions). These combined incentives will be supported by a total budget of USD 4.5 billion.

According to the announcement from the Ministry, the aim of this funding call is to increase Türkiye's production capacity to meet domestic demand and capitalize on the export potential, particularly to the European market. The goal is to establish a total production capacity of 80 GWh, focusing on local content and including investments in R&D centres. The call will support investments in battery and active material production facilities with a minimum annual capacity of 5 GWh, starting from the cell level.

Funding was announced as part of the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, a government initiative aimed at attracting high-value-added investments in key technology sectors. This funding call is one of six announced by the Ministry for different priority sectors (see related state acts). It is stated that more funding calls will be released under the HIT-30 program.

In this context, Minister of Industry and Technology Mehmet Fatih Kacır said: “Under the HIT-30 High Technology Incentive Program, we aim to attract global investments to Turkey, especially in the fields of innovative emerging technologies. Through the first six calls, we intend to make Turkey a production hub for next-generation energy vehicles, battery technologies, chip technologies, solar cells, and wind turbines. Through these six calls, we will attract more than USD 20 billion in private sector investments to our country by 2030" (author's own translation).

interventions.title: Australia: The Queensland government announces the Queensland Critical Minerals Strategy
interventions.description:
On 27 June 2023, the Queensland government announced an investment allocation of AUD 75 million (approx. USD 49.9 million) for establishing critical mineral zones under the Queensland Critical Minerals Strategy. The government notes that these zones will "leverage collaboration opportunities to achieve time, resource and capital efficiencies" betwen projects located in one regional area. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take.

The measure is part of a broader set of investments under the strategy which includes various investments (please see related state acts). The strategy outlines four objectives in an attempt to achieve Queensland’s aspirations for a prosperous critical minerals sector and to deliver the critical minerals needed for a net-zero emissions future. These objectives entail the following:

  • Move faster, smarter
  • Maximise investment
  • Build value chains
  • Foster research and environmental, social and governance (ESG) excellence

Based on the four objectives included, the strategy will oversee an AUD 245 million (approx. USD 163.7 million) investment into growing Queensland’s critical mineral sector. The other interventions did not meet the GTA reporting criteria.

In this context, Resources Minister Scott Stewart said: "Queensland has vast reserves of critical minerals, such as copper, zinc, vanadium and cobalt, that the world is demanding to produce batteries and renewable energy as part of their plans to decarbonise. The Queensland Critical Minerals Strategy is about acting now to position Queensland as a global leader in this new industrial revolution. If we do not manufacture our future, someone else will, leaving Queensland to remain a low-end supplier of minerals and nothing more".

interventions.title: Russia: Government provides RUB 1.54 billion to support a copper mining and processing facility
interventions.description:
On 21 June 2024, the government of the Russian Federation announced RUB 1.54 billion (USD 17.5 million) in state aid to Udokan Copper. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. 

The government funds will support the construction of a mining and processing plant extracting and processing copper ore and the production of cathode copper and commercial sulphide concentrate. This specific allocation will fund part of the main project, namely, the "transport and warehouse complex". 

Udokan Copper (formerly Baikal Mining Company) is a firm established in 2008 with the objective of developing the Udokan Copper Deposit in the Kalar District of the Trans-Baikal Region. 

In this context, Mikhail Mishustin, the Prime Minister of Russia, said: “We expect that this decision will facilitate the influx of private investment, primarily in sectors key to economic development, and improve the lives of our citizens in the Far East".

Udokan Copper will receive further state aid under the decree for a different part of the project (see related state act). In the same decree, the government allocated state aid for several other projects (see related state acts).

interventions.title: Russia: Government provides around RUB 1.6 billion to support a metallurgical factory
interventions.description:

Udokan Copper (formerly Baikal Mining Company) is a firm established in 2008 with the objective of developing the Udokan Copper Deposit in the Kalar District of the Trans-Baikal Region.

interventions.title: Indonesia: Government modified the export tax rules on certain minerals and crude palm oil
interventions.description:
On 3 June 2024, the Minister of Finance of Indonesia issued Regulation No. 38/2024, increasing the export tax on the following minerals:

  • copper concentrate with content of ≥ 15% (HS code 260300) from 0% and 5% depending on the smelter development to 7.5%;
  • laterite iron concentrate (gutite, hematite, magnetite) with a rate ≥ 50% (more than or equal to fifty per cent) Fe and ≥ 10% (Al2O3+SiO2) (HS codes 260111 and 260112) from 0% and 5% depending on the smelter development to 5%; 
  • lead concentrate with levels ≥ 56% Pb (HS code 260700 ) from 0% and 5% depending on the smelter development to 5%; and
  • zinc concentrate with a content of ≥ 51% Zn (HS code 260800) from 0% and 5% depending on the smelter development to 5%.

The new regulation removed the smelter development requirement for determining export duties. 

According to the regulation, the objective is to support the downstream mineral industry through the use of refining facilities in Indonesia.

The previous export duties were regulated under the Minister of Finance Regulation No.39/2022 (see related state act).

The regulation entered into force on 3 June 2024.

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Defense awards South32 a contract to support the production of battery-grade manganese
interventions.description:
On 17 May 2024, the US Department of Defense (DOD) awarded South32 a USD 20 million contract to support the production of battery-grade manganese. Funding will be provided through the Defense Production Act Investment (DPAI) Program to support South32's Hermosa Project in Arizona. Funding will be provided in the form of a Cooperative Agreement.

According to the DOD announcement, the funding will enable South32 to accelerate the production of battery-grade manganese at the Hermosa Project in Santa Cruz County, Arizona. This project will help reduce import dependency for manganese, a critical material used in batteries for both defense and civilian applications. The acceleration will deliver ore up to two years earlier than originally planned, making South32 the first sustainable domestic producer of this essential material.

In this context, Mr. Anthony Di Stasio, acting Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Industrial Base Resilience said: “This first DPA award for manganese production is a big step towards reducing import dependency throughout the battery material supply chain.”

The award was made through the Defense Production Act Investment (DPAI) Program, which focuses on strengthening domestic production capabilities for key materials. This initiative is part of the broader effort by the Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Industrial Base Policy (OASD(IBP)) to ensure resilient and secure supply chains for critical defense materials. The DPAI Program also supports the National Defense Industrial Strategy (see related state act).

A cooperative agreement is a federal funding mechanism similar to a grant, but with more active involvement from the government, offering not just funds but also technical assistance and guidance to the recipient organization.

interventions.title: United States of America: Introduction of tax credits worth USD 19.6 million for American Battery Technology Company under the Advanced Energy Project Credit Program
interventions.description:
On 19 April 2024, the Department of Energy awarded American Battery Technology Company an investment tax credit worth USD 19.6 million. This funding will support the company in building a facility for recycling lithium-ion batteries and producing critical materials. The funding was provided through the Advanced Energy Project Credit Program.

According to the company-provided project description, American Battery Technology Company is building a facility for recycling lithium-ion batteries with a capacity of 20'000 tonnes per year. This facility will produce critical materials such as nickel sulphate, cobalt sulphate, and lithium hydroxide, which are reintroduced into the U.S. supply chain.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “Through the historic Advanced Energy Project Credit, we are leveraging the infrastructure, expertise, and grit of America’s energy communities—where the workers that powered our energy past, will power our energy future.”

U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Janet Yellen said: “Investments in advanced energy projects strengthen our energy security and create good-paying jobs in vital fields like clean energy manufacturing and critical materials processing. They also allow for existing energy infrastructure to be upgraded for the clean energy economy. All of this work will help lower energy costs for American families and small businesses.”

Established by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act and later expanded with a USD 10 billion investment through the Inflation Reduction Act, the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) Program provides tax credits for investments in advanced energy projects (see related state acts). These projects support clean energy manufacturing and recycling and aim to reduce greenhouse gas emissions at industrial facilities. The program addresses a wide range of needs in the clean energy economy, including the production of grid components, electric vehicle parts, and other key technologies. Concurrent with the present project, the Department of Energy announced funding for 35 projects across four categories: Clean Energy and Clean Vehicle Manufacturing, Critical Minerals and Materials, Grid Components and Modernization, and Industrial Decarbonization, with a total allocation of USD 1.93 billion in tax credits.

interventions.title: United States of America: Government prohibits the import of aluminium, copper, and nickel from Russia
interventions.description:
On 12 April 2024, the Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) of the U.S. Department of Treasury issued a determination that imposes an import ban on aluminium, copper, and nickel of Russian origin. The affected HS codes are included under chapters 74, 75 and 76. The decision enters into force on 13 April 2024.

This determination is based on Executive Order 14068 of 11 March 2022 on “Prohibiting Certain Imports, Exports, and New Investment with respect to Continued Russian Federation Aggression”, as amended by Executive Order of 22 December 2023 (“Taking Additional Steps With Respect to the Russian Federation’s Harmful Activities”) (see related state acts).

The Treasury Department made this determination to fulfil the G7 commitments established in February 2024, which aim to disrupt the revenue that Russia earns from its metal exports. Concurrently, the Treasury Department issued another determination that prohibits certain services related to Russia’s metal trading. These actions were taken in coordination with the United Kingdom (see related state acts).

In this context, Secretary of the Treasury Janet L. Yellen said: “Our new prohibitions on key metals, in coordination with our partners in the United Kingdom, will continue to target the revenue Russia can earn to continue its brutal war against Ukraine. By taking this action in a targeted and responsible manner, we will reduce Russia’s earnings while protecting our partners and allies from unwanted spillover effects.”

interventions.title: United States of America (State of Michigan): Approval of Funding for Highland Copper Company’s Copperwood Mine Project in Michigan
interventions.description:
On 26 March 2024, the State of Michigan approved a USD 50 million grant to Highland Copper Company for the construction of the Copperwood Mine project in Gogebic County, Michigan. This project involves underground copper mining operations conducted by the company.

According to the announcement from the Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC), the Copperwood Project, operated by Highland Copper Company through its subsidiary Copperwood, will supply copper critical to the clean tech and mobility industries. The underground copper mining operation in Gogebic County is expected to generate a total capital investment of USD 425 million and create 380 high-wage jobs in the Western Upper Peninsula.

Michigan Economic Development Corporation CEO and Michigan Strategic Fund President and Chair Quentin Messer Jr. said, “Today’s projects illustrate how Team Michigan is executing the ‘Make It in Michigan’ economic development strategy that builds on our advanced manufacturing and R&D successes, celebrates entrepreneurship and innovation, and brings vibrancy to communities across the state. We are grateful to Governor Whitmer, the Michigan Legislature, and local officials for their continued support and collaboration. As Team Michigan, we will keep working to win projects, support vibrant places, and attract and develop our people so that anyone or any business can 'Make It in Michigan'.

Funding was provided through the Michigan Strategic Fund (MSF) under the Strategic Site Readiness Program (SSRP). Administered by the MEDC, SSRP aims to create investment-ready sites across Michigan to attract and support business growth.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces USD 270 million for Wieland’s advanced copper recycling facility
interventions.description:
On 25 March 2024, the Department of Energy announced financial assistance to Wieland North America Recycling for their project called “Advanced Copper Recycling Facility”. This support will help decarbonize the aluminium and metals sector and reduce industrial greenhouse gas emissions. The total value of the funding is approximately USD 270 million and it will be provided in the form of a cooperative agreement.

According to the Department of Energy, the project, led by Wieland North America Recycling, is set to expand the U.S. recycling capacity and capabilities with investments in state-of-the-art copper scrap metal processing technology in Shelbyville, Kentucky. This project aims to enhance the recycling of copper scrap and other metals, enabling the production of high-purity copper that can be used in a variety of applications, such as in the manufacturing of electric vehicles and semiconductors. The project is expected to significantly reduce carbon emissions and contribute to the resilience of the U.S. copper supply chain.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “Spurring on the next generation of decarbonization technologies in key industries like steel, paper, concrete, and glass will keep America the most competitive nation on Earth. Thanks to President Biden’s industrial strategy, DOE is making the largest investment in industrial decarbonization in the history of the United States. These investments will slash emissions from these difficult-to-decarbonize sectors and ensure American businesses and American workers remain at the forefront of the global economy.”

Funding for this project was announced as part of the DOE's Industrial Demonstrations Program (IDP), which was established to fast-track decarbonization efforts in energy-intensive industries. The IDP aims to implement emissions-reducing technologies to set a model for widespread industry adoption and drive significant transformation. Alongside the current award, the DOE also unveiled funding of USD 6 billion for 33 projects spanning eight subsectors: chemicals and refining, cement and concrete, iron and steel, aluminium and metals, food and beverage, glass, process heat, and pulp and paper. This represents the most substantial investment in industrial decarbonization in American history. The announcement for these awards was made in March 2023 (see related state act). The awards under this Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) will be financed through the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) and the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).

A cooperative agreement is a type of federal funding mechanism that is similar to a grant but entails a higher degree of government involvement. Along with providing financial support, it includes technical assistance and guidance for the recipient organization.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces financial assistance to support CO2 utilisation for producing electrolyte components for lithium-ion batteries
interventions.description:
On 25 March 2024, the Department of Energy announced financial assistance to the Dow Chemical Company for their project called “Novel CO2 Utilization for Electric Vehicle Battery Chemical Production”. This support will help decarbonize the chemicals and refining sector. The total value of the funding is approximately USD 95 million and it will be provided in the form of a cooperative agreement.  

According to the Department of Energy, the project led by the Dow Chemical Company is set to construct a facility to capture CO2 from ethylene oxide manufacturing and utilize this CO2 for producing electrolyte components for lithium-ion batteries. This project aims to establish a domestic manufacturing base for expanding U.S. electric vehicle and power storage markets, supporting supply chain resilience and reducing emissions in the chemical production process.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “Spurring on the next generation of decarbonization technologies in key industries like steel, paper, concrete, and glass will keep America the most competitive nation on Earth. interventions.title: United States of America: ARPA-E provides funding to support nickel extraction from soil using plants
interventions.description:
On 21 March 2024, the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) of the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced USD 10 million in funding to support research projects that investigate the feasibility of extraction of nickel in soil by terrestrial plants. Eligible entities for funding include, among others, for-profit entities. Funding will be provided through cooperative agreements and similar agreements.

According to the announcement, ARPA-E is soliciting research on using 'phytomining' to extract nickel cost-effectively and with a low carbon footprint. This process utilizes plants that absorb metals from the soil, creating a bio-ore that can supplement traditional mining, reduce imports, and support clean energy technologies. The goal is to advance phytomining technology, enhance nickel extraction in plants, and explore its use on non-agricultural lands.

In this context, ARPA-E Director Evelyn N. Wang said: “In order to accomplish the goals laid out by President Biden to meet our clean energy targets, and support our economy and national security, it’s going to take all-hands-on-deck approach and innovative solutions. By exploring phytomining to extract nickel as the first target critical material, ARPA-E aims to achieve a cost-competitive and low-carbon footprint extraction approach needed to support the energy transition.”

A cooperative agreement is a federal funding mechanism similar to a grant, but with more active involvement from the government, offering not just funds but also technical assistance and guidance to the recipient organization.

ARPA-E which is an agency under the DOE provides funding for high-potential, high-impact energy technologies that are too early for private-sector investment.

interventions.title: Australia: Government announces USD 533 million funding for Arafula Rare Earths Limited
interventions.description:
On 14 March 2024, the Export Finance Australia (EFA) announced funding in a state loan worth USD 125 million to Arafura Rare Earths Ltd. The funding is to aid the firm's Nolans Project in the northern territory. The funding is a part of the Critical Minerals Facility worth AUD 4 billion (please see related state act). The project will focus on the production of NdPr oxide.

The EFA financing is a part of the total funding worth AUD 840 million provided by the Australian government with further allocations also made by the North Australian Infrastructure Facility (please see related intervention). The Australian Government, however, only made public the funds by the EFA and the NAIF.

In this context, Northern Territory Chief Minister Eva Lawler said: "Arafura’s project is an example of how this Government is attracting investment into our critical minerals sector that will significantly contribute to the Territory’s role in energy transition and reducing greenhouse gas emissions".

interventions.title: Belgium: EIB provides financing for 'UMICORE Battery Materials RDI'
interventions.description:
On 7 February 2024, the European Investment Bank (EIB) and UMICORE signed an agreement worth EUR 350 million (approx. USD 378 million) for the project 'UMICORE Battery Materials RDI' from Belgium. The total cost of the project is estimated at EUR 705 million.

According to the project description issued by the EIB, 'The project will cover the promoter-s RDI programme, supporting electrification-focused projects related to rechargeable battery materials, next generation battery materials and battery recycling solutions'.

The objective of the project is defined as follows: 'The aim is to accelerate the delivery of the company's sustainability strategy, focused on global mobility transformation, by providing solutions to meet the growing need for advanced materials and enable greater circularity for critical metals'.

interventions.title: China: Government subsidies for listed company Yunnan Copper Co.,Ltd in year 2024
interventions.description:

Yunnan Copper Co.,Ltd is a Diversified Metals & Mining enterprise based in Kunming City, Yunnan Province.

In China it is a legal requirement that publicly-listed firms report any subsidies received from government bodies. The stated subsidy amounts refer to a given calendar year. It is possible that this firm received other forms of state aid that have not been declared.

The affected products and sectors have been chosen based on the "main products" and CSRC sector classification reported in the financial data of the firm. The subsidy amounts in USD were computed using the year-average exchange rate to the Chinese Renminbi.
